one_line_thesis: Enterprise cloud HCM/finance compounder with $28.1B RPO and improving margins; hold at ~$230 on 16.38% synthesis IRR (partial dhando).

deep_dive.executive_summary: Workday is an enterprise cloud compounder with a large subscription backlog and improving operating margins. FY2026 free cash flow was approximately $2.78 billion ($10.36 per share on 268.1 million diluted shares), operating income $721 million, and the company repurchased $2.90 billion of stock. At $230.00 per share (NASDAQ close 2026-07-09), we expect 16.38% per year over seven years on filed FY2026 owner cash (Lawrence base 15.5% per year). Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable: switching costs and workflow integration defend the installed base, but Oracle and SAP limit m...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Operating income moved +73.7% versus the prior period (415.0 to 721.0).
Basic EPS moved +31.8% versus the prior period (2.0 to 2.6).
Q1 FY2027: revenue $2.54B (+13.5% YoY); operating income $338M vs $39M prior year quarter
FY2026 10-K: revenue $9.55B (+13%); FCF ~$2.78B; RPO $28.1B; $2.9B share repurchases
Completed Evisort acquisition — AI contract intelligence embedded in Financials suite
Expanded share repurchase authorization; FY2026 repurchased 12.8M shares for $2.9B
Launched Workday Illuminate AI platform across HCM and Financials applications
Acquired HiredScore — AI-powered talent acquisition and internal mobility
Carl Eschenbach sole CEO; co-founder Aneel Bhusri executive chair — leadership transition complete
Carl Eschenbach named co-CEO alongside Aneel Bhusri
Issued $3.0B senior notes; repaid term loan and convertible notes — balance sheet simplification
Acquired Scout RFP — spend management expansion into strategic sourcing
Opened Financial Management cloud beyond HCM — dual-pillar ERP strategy
NYSE IPO — transitioned from private to public cloud ERP vendor

industry.structure: Enterprise cloud HCM and finance software is an oligopoly at the large-enterprise tier (Workday, Oracle, SAP) with mid-market challengers (UKG, ADP, Dayforce). Switching costs and multi-year contracts create high retention but long sales cycles.
industry.share_shift: Workday is gaining share in cloud HCM and finance versus legacy on-premise ERP; Oracle and SAP defend installed bases with hybrid bundles. AI-native point solutions attack recruiting and spend workflows at the edge.
industry.trend: Generative AI is embedding into HR and finance workflows (Illuminate, agentic automation); RPO growth and margin expansion are the owner-cash story; large buybacks signal mature-compounder capital allocation.

risk_claims.claim: Growth regime downshift: YoY growth moved to +12.6% to +13.5% YoY (baseline median +17.6% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
*Primary risk: Oracle and SAP bundle cloud ERP with existing database relationships, slowing Workday net-new finance logos and forcing price concessions on renewals.
Competitive substitution: Incumbent ERP vendors can undercut on total cost of ownership for dual HCM/finance deals (10-K risk factors).
